executiveSummary.text: D-Wave is one of the few listed pure-play quantum names with real commercial customers, but it still trades more on narrative than fundamentals. At $14.56 and a ~$5.4bn market cap, the stock is pricing in a lot of future success today. Bottom line: REDUCE — SPECULATIVE. Real technology, weak valuation support.

businessModel.text: D-Wave sells quantum computing systems, cloud quantum access, and professional services. The key question is whether commercial adoption arrives fast enough to justify the current valuation.

financialSnapshot.text: Price: $14.56 Market cap: ~$5.37bn 52-week range: $5.97 – $46.75 P/E: N/A EPS: -$1.11

recentCatalysts.text: Quantum sector momentum has driven multiple expansion. Commercial proof points remain limited relative to market expectations. Any slowdown in AI/quantum narrative could compress valuation fast.
items: Quantum sector momentum has driven multiple expansion.
Commercial proof points remain limited relative to market expectations.
Any slowdown in AI/quantum narrative could compress valuation fast.

thesisEvaluation.text: Bull (20%): quantum commercialisation accelerates and commercial contracts surprise to the upside. Base (45%): quantum adoption remains slow; valuation stays elevated and range-bound. Bear (35%): funding pressure or data disappointment; equity erodes toward lower support. Weighted conviction: (20% × 70) + (45% × 45) + (35% × 20) = 14 + 20.25 + 7 = 41 → 44/100 .

keyRisks.text: 1 Valuation is far ahead of current fundamentals. 2 Quantum commercial adoption may take longer than hoped. 3 Equity dilution remains a risk for pre-profit technology firms.

risks.risk: 1 Valuation is far ahead of current fundamentals.

recommendation.text: REDUCE — SPECULATIVE. Interesting exposure, poor risk/reward after the rerating.
